annotation_status: draft — requires validation by domain expert before production use
phase: Phase 2 (4-6 weeks) — starter set; target is 15-20 cases
task_description: Given a new article and a corpus of 10 past articles, identify which past articles provide meaningful context for understanding the new article. Rank context articles by relevance and explain their role (direct precursor, regulatory precedent, competitive parallel, etc.).

input_format.corpus: 10 past articles in chronological order (mix of relevant and irrelevant)

output_format.relevant_context_ids: IDs of corpus articles that provide meaningful context
output_format.context_roles: Per-article explanation: role type + explanation of why it is relevant
output_format.not_relevant_ids: IDs correctly excluded with brief rationale

context_role_taxonomy: direct_precursor — same story, earlier in time
story_development — same story, concurrent or later development
competitive_parallel — same product/strategy at a competitor
financial_context — financial results that explain the underlying economics
regulatory_precedent — earlier legal/regulatory case that enables or frames this one
proximate_trigger — the specific event that caused the article_new event to happen
governance_context — shareholder or board pressure that explains company behaviour
legal_context — court ruling or legal proceeding directly relevant

jtbd15-001.chain: Agent open letter (Feb) → Tiered subscription launch (Feb) → FY2025 results (Feb) → Czech precedent (Mar) → Lawsuit filed (Apr)
jtbd15-001.key_test: Does model reconstruct the causal chain or only retrieve the most similar surface-level article?

jtbd15-002.chain: Divest adjacent businesses (Feb) → Confirm fold-in plan (Feb) → Eliminate local teams (Mar)
jtbd15-002.key_test: Does model identify 3-step consolidation sequence with shareholder pressure context?

jtbd15-003.chain: Redfin-Compass alliance (Feb) → Court ruling (Feb) → Zillow Preview launch (Mar) → Homes.com parallel launch (Mar) → Traction update (Mar)
jtbd15-003.key_test: Does model understand that Homes.com parallel (c04) is critical competitive context?

corpus_design_principles: Each corpus contains 10 articles: a mix of highly relevant, moderately relevant, and clearly irrelevant
Irrelevant articles are plausible distractors (same sector, different story) not random noise
Articles span 2 months before the new article to test temporal reasoning
At least one corpus always includes a competitive parallel (same product at a competitor)

evaluation_metrics.precision_at_k: What fraction of retrieved articles are truly relevant (k=3, k=5)?
evaluation_metrics.recall: What fraction of golden relevant articles are retrieved?
evaluation_metrics.role_accuracy: For retrieved relevant articles, does the model correctly identify the context role?
evaluation_metrics.narrative_coherence: LLM-as-judge: does the model's context explanation reconstruct the causal chain? (0-5 rubric)

narrative_coherence_rubric.5: Reconstructs full causal chain, identifies all context roles correctly, explains missing context explicitly
narrative_coherence_rubric.4: Identifies all high-relevance articles, explains their roles, misses some medium-relevance pieces
narrative_coherence_rubric.3: Retrieves most relevant articles but role descriptions are shallow or partially incorrect
narrative_coherence_rubric.2: Retrieves only the most obvious context (direct precursor) and misses regulatory/competitive context
narrative_coherence_rubric.1: Retrieves surface-level similar articles only (e.g. same company, wrong story)

data_sources: All articles derived from .state/raw/feb-collected.json and .state/raw/collected-all.json with leads reconstructed from summaries
avito_relevance_notes: jtbd15-002 (CoStar/Domain consolidation) is the highest-Avito-relevance case — the pattern of a dominant player centralising acquired portal operations is directly applicable to Avito's ecosystem strategy.
